title: Sort: Numeric Ascending
description: Given an array of numbers, return the array sorted in ascending order. Use a comparison function to ensure numeric sorting (not string sorting).
whatShouldHappen: Provide a comparison function `(a, b) => a - b`.
patternExplanation: By default, .sort() converts elements to strings. A comparator (a, b) => a - b is required for numeric order.

title: Sort: Numeric Descending
description: Given an array of numbers, return the array sorted in descending order (largest to smallest).
whatShouldHappen: Provide a comparison function `(a, b) => b - a`.
patternExplanation: Flipping the comparator logic to (b - a) results in a descending sort.

title: Sort: Objects by Property
description: Given an array of user objects `{ name: string, age: number }`, sort the array by age in ascending order.
patternExplanation: When sorting objects, the comparator must access the specific property you want to sort by.

title: Sort: Case-Insensitive Strings
description: Given an array of strings, return them sorted alphabetically, regardless of case (e.g., 'a' should come before 'B'). Default string sort puts all uppercase before lowercase.
whatShouldHappen: Compare the lowercase versions of strings `a` and `b`.
Use `localeCompare()` for a robust string comparison, or manually check `<` and `>`.
patternExplanation: Normalizing strings to lowercase before comparing ensures consistent alphabetical order without ASCII value bias.

title: Sort: Multiple Criteria
description: Given an array of player objects `{ name: string, score: number }`, sort them first by score in DESCENDING order. If scores are tied, sort those players by name in ASCENDING alphabetical order.
whatShouldHappen: The comparator should first calculate `b.score - a.score`.
If that difference is not 0, return it.
If the difference is 0 (scores are equal), return the result of comparing `a.name` and `b.name` using `localeCompare()`.
patternExplanation: Chaining comparison logic allows you to define complex priority rules for sorting.

title: Reverse: Array Reversal
description: Given an array, reverse the order of its elements in-place using `.reverse()`.
patternExplanation: .reverse() mutates the original array and reverses it. It is very fast and efficient.

title: Sort: By String Length
description: Given an array of strings, sort them by their length in ascending order (shortest to longest).
patternExplanation: Sorting by a derived value (like length) follows the same pattern as sorting by a property.
